birds.evidence: thumb|Drawing of the head of a stubble quail The stubble quail (Coturnix pectoralis), whose other common names include grey quail and pectoral quail, is a species of Old World quail native to Australia. The species is widespread in mainland Australia and is listed as Least Concern by the IUCN,{{cite web|author=Anonymous

birds.evidence: | genus = Coturnix | species = japonica | authority = Temminck & Schlegel, 1848 }} The Japanese quail (Coturnix japonica), also known as the coturnix quail, is a species of Old World quail found in East Asia
